questions.stem: This H&E-stained liver biopsy shows numerous large, clear, round vacuoles that fill the hepatocytes and displace their nuclei to the cell periphery. What do these vacuoles most likely represent?

explanation.summary: In hepatic steatosis, triglyceride droplets coalesce into a single large vacuole that pushes the nucleus to the periphery, producing macrovesicular fat. Zone 3 (centrilobular) accentuation is characteristic of adult MASLD.
explanation.whyCorrect: In hepatic steatosis, triglyceride droplets coalesce into a single large vacuole that pushes the nucleus to the periphery, producing macrovesicular fat. Zone 3 (centrilobular) accentuation is characteristic of adult MASLD.
